task.name: Project Learnings
task.objective: Capture, search, and manage cross-session project learnings

llm.mandate: Learnings persist across sessions in JSONL format
Always search before building — check if this pattern was seen before
Each learning entry has: key, type, insight, files, source, ts

protocol.name: learnings-search
protocol.desc: Search project learnings for relevant context

check.action: Search entries by relevance to the current task context: - Match against file paths being modified - Match against technology/framework keywords - Match against the current workflow type (code-review, dev-story, etc.)
Return the top 5 most relevant entries (by recency + relevance)
Continue silently — no learnings to surface

protocol.name: learnings-capture
protocol.desc: Record a learning from the current workflow
protocol.action: Create {project-root}/team/_memory/_learnings/ directory if it does not exist

Construct learning entry as JSON: { "key": "{{short-kebab-case-key}}", "type": "{{pattern|pitfall|decision|architecture}}", "insight": "{{one-line description of what was learned}}", "files": ["{{list of relevant file paths}}"], "source": "{{workflow that captured this: code-review, dev-story, etc.}}", "ts": "{{current ISO timestamp}}" }
Append the JSON entry as a single line to the learnings JSONL file

protocol.name: learnings-prune
protocol.action: Read all entries from the learnings JSONL file
For each entry with a "files" field: - Check if those files still exist in the project - If ALL referenced files are deleted, mark as STALE
For entries with the same "key": - If insights contradict, mark as CONFLICT - The most recent entry wins (latest timestamp)
Report stale and conflicting entries for user decision
